4 files

Can you design the drainage system?

Of course, let me analyse the project requirements and regulations.

19 steps
⌃
  • Loading project context...
  • Location identified: London, UK.
  • Checking Approved Document H.
  • Checking BS EN 12056-2 for gravity drainage.
  • Checking BS EN 12056-3 for roof drainage.
  • Reviewing local water authority requirements.
  • Loading company standard.
  • Checking similar project approaches.
  • Client preference found: SVPs to be boxed in risers.
  • Finding rooms from Revit model...
  • 24 rooms found requiring drainage.
  • Checking number of sanitary outlets per room.
  • Calculating discharge units per BS EN 12056.
  • Checking for additional drainage requirements.
  • Reading project specification...
  • Calculating condensate connection requirements.
  • Looking for civil engineering pop-up information...
  • ✕Civil data not found in project folder.
  • Stopped — awaiting civil data.

I've analysed the project against Approved Document H and BS EN 12056. Local authority requirements and similar projects checked. Client preference for boxed SVPs noted.

However, I need the civil engineering information to locate the drainage pop-ups. Do you have this available?

Yes, sorry just added to the folder now.

8 steps
⌃
  • Continuing analysis...
  • Reading civil engineering drawings.
  • Parsing drainage pop-up locations from civil data.
  • Cross-referencing with room layout requirements.
  • Pop-up locations differ from optimal positions.
  • Checking if relocation is feasible per Approved Doc H.
  • ✎Placing pop-up markers on drawing for review.+366drainage-layout.rvt
  • Analysis complete.

I've cross-referenced the civil pop-up locations with the room requirements. Some positions differ from optimal — I've marked these for your review.

Ready to proceed with the full drainage design? Type yes or proceed to continue.

Type "yes" or "proceed" to generate drainage layout
∞ Build ▾
Mode
∞ Build Full autonomous design mode - creates and places elements
☰ Designer Designer mode - outlines steps before executing
⚙ QA QA Mode - identifies and fixes issues in existing designs
? Query Query the model for answers and golden thread decision making logic
BUILD X. 0.1 ▾
Model
BUILD X.0.1✓Advanced premium model incorporating every service
BUILD MEP.0.1✓Production-ready model optimized for mechanical, electrical and plumbing systems
BUILD A.0.1✓Architectural coordination model for space planning and layout optimization
LLM Mode ⚠

Chat GPT 5.2
Gemini 3.0

Included in Revit CoPilot, an array of commands for design and modelling.

573+ Commands Exposed Revit commands from the chat — every routine MEP task
561 MCP Tools Context, read, write and action tools for the AI agent
Create your own Commands Build custom commands and workflows from the UI
Connect your own plugins Exposed tool calling — wire your own modules into the Copilot
QA Manager Continuous quality assurance against your project standards
CoBie Manager BIM hand-over data — validated, exported, ready for the client
Document Controller Transmittals, registers, revision tracking from the model
Drawing Production Sheets, views, annotations, titleblocks — automated from chat
AI Model Set up Worksets, levels, grids, templates — project initialisation in one prompt
AI Notes Automated annotation and markup directly on the model
AI Smart Tagging Intelligent element tagging — rooms, equipment, services

Roadmap
See the full roadmap →

Clash Solver

Ingests your ACC clash report, proposes a resolution for every clash, and applies the ones you accept — straight into the live model.

Stops reporting clashes. Starts solving them.

Connect Clash Solver to your ACC project. It pulls the latest clash report, deduplicates against the previous run, and triages: real clash vs ghost (model-link drift, family swap, view-filter mismatch). For each real clash it proposes a resolution — re-route the smaller service, raise the ceiling void in the affected zone, swap a fitting, etc. — and shows you what the model would look like after.

You accept, refine, or reject each proposal. Accepted resolutions are applied to the live model as auditable Revit transactions (one-click undo). Rejected ones go into a 'why we kept this' log so the next sync doesn't propose the same thing.

Open Clash Solver →

COBie Manager

BIM hand-over data validated, structured and exported — without the spreadsheet marathon.

Data Handover

COBie deliverables from the live model

Extract asset registers, space data, system breakdowns and maintenance schedules directly from the Revit model. Every row validated against the COBie schema before it leaves the building.

No manual mapping. No copy-paste from schedules. The agent reads the model, fills the workbook, flags what's missing, and hands you a file ready for the client.

Open COBie Manager →
COBie Webview

Templates you own

Create your own templates — sell your own templates. Two authoring engines that turn your standards into repeatable deliverables.

Report Builder

Automated technical reports from model data

Define a report template once — cover page, tables, charts, appendices. Point it at a project and the agent populates every section from live model data, calculation results and QA logs.

Export to PDF or Word. Rebrand it, resell it, or keep it for your own QA pipeline.

Open Report Builder →
Report preview
Specbuilder

Technical specifications written from the model

Select a system, pick a specification standard, and the agent writes clause-by-clause from your project data. NBS, CIBSE, ASHRAE or your own house template.

Version-controlled, diff-tracked, and always in sync with what's actually in the model.

Open Specbuilder →
Spec preview

Agentic modules

Managed services where Adelphos runs the agent against your business — finances, project management, document control.

Managed service

Financial Forecasting

Cash-flow projections, cost tracking and variance analysis pulled from your live project data every week.

Managed service

Project Management Agent

Programme tracking, milestone alerts and resource utilisation — compiled from model progress and team logs.

Managed service

Document Control

Transmittals, revision registers and distribution logs — generated, tracked and archived automatically.

Managed service

Procurement Agent

Material take-offs, supplier quotes and purchase order tracking driven by the model bill of quantities.

Managed service

Resource Planning

Workforce allocation, subcontractor scheduling and equipment bookings aligned to the live programme.

Managed service

Compliance Monitoring

Regulatory checks, certification tracking and audit trails — flagged before a deadline is missed.

Calculator engines

Verified calculation modules that the agent calls during design. Each engine runs server-side, returns auditable results.

SAP EPC SBEM Cable Calculations Lighting Calculations Thermal Calculations Pipe & Duct Sizing

No data stored, no chase-ups, no spam. All uploaded data lives on an AWS / Azure backend — we don't see it. Your models, your documents, your calculations stay yours.

Ready to put Adelphos to work?

Most of the platform is self-serve. The agentic modules need a 30-minute walkthrough.

Try an app → Talk to us

Drag to rotate · scroll to zoom · right-click to pan
